PS C:\Users\ADITHYA\OneDrive\Desktop\Cora antig\cora> 
what is this error

Error:
Git failed to push to origin because updates were rejected.

Cause:
Another repository has pushed changes to the same branch that you are trying to push to, so Git does not allow you to push your changes until you pull the latest changes from the remote repository and then merge them into your local branch.

Fix:
1. Run `git pull` command to get the latest updates from the remote repository.
2. Resolve any conflicts that may arise due to the updates.
3. Once all conflicts have been resolved, run `git push -u origin main` command again to push your changes to origin.

Commands:
```bash
$ git pull
# resolve conflicts if any
$ git push -u origin main
```
USER: can you give the command for git push

Sure, here is the command for `git push`:
```sql
git push <remote_name> <branch_name>
```
Replace `<remote_name>` with the name of the remote repository that you want to push your changes to (e.g., origin) and `<branch_name>` with the name of the branch that you want to push (e.g., main).

Error:
The Git push command failed because there were updates from another repository that you do not have locally. This is a common error when trying to push changes to a branch that has already been updated by another user.

Cause:
The changes made by another user have been merged into your

To get the most recent version of a repository from a remote server, you can use the `git pull` command in Git. This will download any new commits and updates that have been added to the repository since the last time you pulled changes. Here's an example command:
```sql
$ git pull origin main
```
Replace `origin` with the name of your remote server if it's different, and replace `main` with the name of the branch you want to update (which is typically the default branch called `master` or `main`).

After running this command, Git will download any new changes and merge them into your local repository. You can then push your changes to the remote server using the `git push` command as usual:
```sql
$ git push origin main
```
Again, replace `origin` with the name of your remote server if it's different, and replace `main` with the name of the branch you want to update.
